rulings.Case Title: KEVIN AYERS ET AL VS. JEFF CREAR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion For Judgment Pursuant To Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 19, 2020 line 1. PLAINTIFFS KEVIN AYERS, JANICE SUKAITIS' Motion For Judgment Pursuant To Settlement Agreement For Judgment Pursuant To Settlement Agreement Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail addresses provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion for Judgment pursuant to Stipulation is GRANTED as requested. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE MALOY VS. FRANK STONICH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 19, 2020 line 2. DEFENDANT FRANK STONICH DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail addresses provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is continued to August 26, 2020. Defendant's demurrer advances several distinct and independent arguments seeking an order sustaining the demurrer without leave to amend. Plaintiff's opposition by not addressing the argument advanced by the Defendant in section D of the Points and Authorities, appears to be conceding the issue. If opposition was intentionally omitted. the Court will sustain the demurrer based on Defendant's unopposed argument without leave to amend. If Plaintiff inadvertently omitted opposition to this issue, Plaintiff shall (1) file a declaration regarding the omission; and (2) file a supplemental brief addressing section D of Defendant's moving Points and Authorities, not to exceed 3 pages. Supplemental filings described above to be filed and served no later than 5:00 p.m. on August 19, 2020. Defendant may file a supplemental reply, not to exceed 3 pages, by noon on August 24, 2020. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BARNEY DIAMOS VS. KELLY P. RYAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 19, 2020 line 4. DEFENDANT KELLY RYAN'S Motion To Set Aside Default Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail addresses provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion to Set Aside Default is DENIED. The motion appears to be seeking mandatory relief under CCP 473(b), however it is not accompanied by "an attorney's sworn affidavit attesting to his or her mistake, inadvertence, surprise or neglect" as required by CCP 473(b). = (501/CFH)
